summary: Problem 3 asks for the construction of a genuinely novel nontrivial Markov chain on restricted distinct-part partitions whose stationary distribution is expressed via interpolation ASEP and interpolation Macdonald polynomials at q=1. The current state shows no solution.tex was produced; the interactive solve was stopped without a result, and the low 13% solvability score reflects the open-ended, construction-heavy nature of the task. No verifiable proof artifact currently exists.
problems: No solution.tex was generated — the solver-agent stopped the interactive solve without producing any candidate proof or construction.
The core mathematical blocker is the absence of an explicit Markov chain definition: no transition rules, state-space dynamics, or reversibility/detailed-balance argument have been proposed.

The stationary distribution must be matched to a ratio of interpolation ASEP polynomials and interpolation Macdonald polynomials at q=1 — specialization behavior at q=1 and the combinatorial interpretation of these objects on S_n(lambda) are not yet worked out.
The 'restricted partition' constraints (unique part of size 0, no part of size 1) impose structural conditions on the state space that have not been analyzed for closure under any proposed chain.
Conflicting status signals: best proof reports '100 open issues' while the issue list marks the proof issue as 'resolved', indicating tracking inconsistency rather than actual mathematical progress.
highlights: The problem is well-scoped with explicit hypotheses (distinct parts, restricted condition) providing a concrete, finite state space S_n(lambda) to work over.

The connection to known objects (interpolation Macdonald polynomials, ASEP) means existing literature by Williams and collaborators offers a strong template for the multiline-queue / Markov-chain construction approach.
The q=1 specialization is a natural simplification point that may admit a cleaner combinatorial stationary distribution than the full q,t case.

suggested_todos.title: Define a candidate Markov chain explicitly: specify state space S_n(lambda), transition moves respecting the restricted condition, and transition probabilities.
Compute or characterize the target stationary distribution as the ratio of interpolation ASEP and interpolation Macdonald polynomials specialized at q=1.
Attempt a detailed-balance / reversibility verification or an eigenvector argument linking the chain's stationary measure to the target ratio.
Review Williams et al. ASEP–Macdonald polynomial and multiline-queue literature to adapt an existing chain construction to the restricted distinct-part setting.
Reconcile conflicting issue-tracking metadata (resolved status vs. 100 open issues) and confirm no partial artifact was lost from the stopped interactive solve.
Produce an initial solution.tex skeleton documenting the problem setup and any partial construction to enable incremental progress.
